metadata.name: cds-core-security-rules
metadata.description: Core security validation rules for cds validate --security

rules.title: Hardcoded secret-like value
rules.message: Secret-like value appears to be hardcoded
rules.whyItMatters: Hardcoded secrets are easy to leak via git, logs, and rendered artifacts.
recommendation: Move the value to environment variables or a secret backend.
Replace literal values with references.

rules.title: Secret embedded in URL or DSN
rules.message: Credential material appears embedded in a connection string or URL
rules.whyItMatters: Secrets in URLs are commonly exposed through logs, process listings, and config snapshots.
recommendation: Move credentials out of DSNs and inject them separately.
Avoid query-string tokens in configuration.

rules.title: Inline private key or high-entropy secret material
rules.message: Private key material or secret-like high-entropy value appears inline
rules.whyItMatters: Inline key material is highly sensitive and difficult to control once committed or rendered.
recommendation: Store private keys in a secret backend or mounted secret file.
Do not keep cryptographic material directly in profile YAML.

rules.title: Default or placeholder secret in non-local profile
rules.message: Weak default or placeholder secret value detected
rules.whyItMatters: Default credentials are a common first target and are frequently exploited automatically.
recommendation: Generate unique secrets for each environment.
Reject placeholder values outside local-only demo profiles.

rules.title: Secret interpolated into non-secret field
rules.message: Secret reference appears to flow into a non-secret sink
rules.whyItMatters: Non-secret fields are often surfaced in logs, metadata, or UI output.
recommendation: Restrict secrets to approved secret sinks such as env refs or secret files.
Do not inject secrets into labels, command arguments, or annotations.

rules.title: Resolved secret present in generated or CLI output
rules.message: Rendered or planned output appears to contain a resolved secret
rules.whyItMatters: Generated artifacts are often stored, shared, or captured by CI systems.
recommendation: Redact secrets in plan and render output.
Avoid writing resolved secret values to generated files.

rules.title: Default admin credentials
rules.message: Known default administrative credentials detected
rules.whyItMatters: Attackers routinely try default credentials first.
recommendation: Set unique admin credentials before deployment.
Disallow well-known defaults in validation.

rules.title: Missing required authentication secret
rules.message: A required authentication secret appears missing or empty
rules.whyItMatters: Missing auth secrets commonly trigger insecure defaults or prevent security controls from working.
recommendation: Provide the required secret through environment or a secret store.
Fail validation when required auth material is missing.

rules.title: Weak secret strength
rules.message: Secret value appears too weak or predictable
rules.whyItMatters: Short or predictable secrets can be guessed or brute-forced easily.
recommendation: Require randomly generated secrets of sufficient length.
Reject dictionary-like or commonly used values.

rules.title: Secret reused across unrelated services
rules.message: The same secret appears reused across multiple services
rules.whyItMatters: Credential reuse increases blast radius if one service is compromised.
recommendation: Use separate credentials or secrets per service.
Generate scoped secrets rather than sharing one across components.

rules.title: Admin or UI service bound to 0.0.0.0
rules.message: Administrative or UI service is exposed on all interfaces
rules.whyItMatters: Broad exposure increases the chance of unintended access from other hosts or networks.
recommendation: Bind local admin services to 127.0.0.1 by default.
Require explicit opt-in for broad network exposure.

rules.title: Database service published externally
rules.message: Database service is published to the host network
rules.whyItMatters: Direct database exposure significantly expands attack surface.
recommendation: Avoid publishing database ports unless strictly necessary.
Use internal networking or localhost-only bindings.

rules.title: Non-local interface exposure in local profile
rules.message: A local profile exposes a service beyond localhost
rules.whyItMatters: Local demo environments are often assumed private but may still be reachable from nearby networks.
recommendation: Prefer 127.0.0.1 bindings in local profiles.
Require an explicit allow override for broader exposure.

rules.title: Authenticated service uses plain HTTP
rules.message: Authenticated or user-facing service appears configured without TLS
rules.whyItMatters: Plain HTTP can expose credentials or session material over the network.
recommendation: Use HTTPS/TLS where the service is accessed over a network.
Document exceptions for local-only usage.

rules.title: Generated file contains resolved secrets
rules.message: Generated file appears to contain secret material
rules.whyItMatters: Files written to disk are easy to copy, commit, back up, or read later by unintended users.
recommendation: Redact generated output or write secret references only.
Keep rendered artifacts free of resolved secrets.

rules.title: Env file path appears repository-managed or unmanaged
rules.message: Environment file path may be stored or handled insecurely
rules.whyItMatters: Env files often accumulate sensitive data and are easily committed accidentally.
recommendation: Keep secret env files out of generated output directories and version control.
Prefer secret injection over checked-in env files.

rules.title: Secret file permissions too broad
rules.message: Secret-bearing file permissions are broader than owner-only
rules.whyItMatters: Local users or processes may read secrets that should be restricted.
recommendation: Set permissions to owner-only for files containing secrets.
Tighten file creation umask for generated secret artifacts.

rules.title: Literal secret stored in profile instead of secret source
rules.message: A secret-like field contains a literal value rather than an external reference
rules.whyItMatters: Profiles are commonly shared, rendered, or checked into source control.
recommendation: Use environment or secret-backend references for sensitive fields.
Keep profiles declarative and secret-free.

rules.title: Unresolved env var falls back to insecure default
rules.message: Environment interpolation uses an insecure fallback default
rules.whyItMatters: Fallbacks silently weaken security when required env vars are missing.
recommendation: Remove insecure defaults for secrets.
Fail validation when mandatory secret env vars are absent.

rules.title: Required env var resolves to empty secret
rules.message: Required secret-like field resolves to an empty value
rules.whyItMatters: Empty secrets may disable protection or trigger insecure application behavior.
recommendation: Ensure required env vars are present before rendering.
Treat empty secret values as validation failures.

rules.title: Binding injects secret into unapproved target
rules.message: Binding appears to route secret material into a forbidden sink
rules.whyItMatters: Incorrect binding targets can leak secrets to metadata, arguments, or unrelated services.
recommendation: Allow secrets only in approved sinks such as environment references or secret mounts.
Add policy checks for binding destinations.

rules.title: Profile inheritance weakens secure defaults
rules.message: Derived profile appears to override a secure parent setting with an insecure value
rules.whyItMatters: Inheritance can unintentionally remove protections when child profiles diverge.
recommendation: Track origin of inherited settings and flag security regressions.
Require explicit approval for overrides that reduce security.

rules.title: Container image uses latest tag
rules.message: Container image uses the latest tag
rules.whyItMatters: Floating tags reduce reproducibility and can introduce unexpected changes.
recommendation: Pin images to explicit versions.
Prefer immutable digests for critical services.

rules.title: Critical image missing digest pin
rules.message: Critical service image is not pinned by digest
rules.whyItMatters: Digest pinning helps ensure integrity and repeatable deployments.
recommendation: Use image digests for critical services.
Define policy exceptions only when necessary.

rules.title: Image registry outside allowlist
rules.message: Image registry does not match the configured allowlist
rules.whyItMatters: Unreviewed registries increase supply-chain risk and reduce provenance confidence.
recommendation: Restrict images to trusted registries.
Maintain an explicit allowlist per environment.

rules.title: Image version below policy minimum
rules.message: Image tag may violate minimum supported version policy
rules.whyItMatters: Older images are more likely to contain known vulnerabilities or unsupported components.
recommendation: Define minimum supported versions for critical services.
Fail validation when image tags fall below policy.

rules.title: Unpinned image tag in non-local profile
rules.message: Service image uses the ':latest' tag in a non-local profile
rules.whyItMatters: Unpinned images can silently introduce breaking changes or vulnerabilities.
recommendation: Pin images to a specific version tag or digest.
Use digest pinning (@sha256:...) for production environments.

rules.title: Container runs as root
rules.message: Container appears configured to run as root
rules.whyItMatters: Running as root increases impact if the container is compromised.
recommendation: Run services as a non-root user where supported.
Define user IDs explicitly in hardened profiles.

rules.title: Privileged container or excessive capabilities
rules.message: Container requests privileged mode or broad kernel capabilities
rules.whyItMatters: Privileged execution significantly increases host compromise risk.
recommendation: Disable privileged mode unless strictly required.
Grant the minimum capabilities necessary.

rules.title: Writable root filesystem
rules.message: Service does not enforce a read-only root filesystem
rules.whyItMatters: Writable roots make persistence and tampering easier after compromise.
recommendation: Enable read-only root filesystems where feasible.
Use explicit writable mounts for required paths.

rules.title: Sensitive host path mount
rules.message: Service mounts a sensitive host path
rules.whyItMatters: Sensitive host mounts can expose control of the host or critical credentials.
recommendation: Remove sensitive host mounts unless essential.
Use narrower read-only mounts and isolate access.

rules.title: Secret appears in command args or log configuration
rules.message: Secret-like material appears in command-line arguments or logging config
rules.whyItMatters: Arguments and logging settings are commonly exposed through process listings and diagnostics.
recommendation: Pass secrets through environment references or mounted secret files.
Avoid including secret values in command-line flags.

rules.title: CLI plan or validate output prints secrets
rules.message: CLI output appears to contain sensitive values
rules.whyItMatters: Terminal output is often copied into tickets, CI logs, or chat systems.
recommendation: Redact sensitive output by default.
Provide explicit unsafe flags for debugging only.

rules.title: Debug mode enabled for sensitive service
rules.message: Debug or overly verbose logging appears enabled
rules.whyItMatters: Debug modes frequently expose configuration values, stack traces, or credentials.
recommendation: Disable debug logging in shared or sensitive environments.
Use controlled debug overrides only when necessary.
